Scenario.overview: Communications Intermediate A skilled threat actor launched a multi-stage attack targeting a communications provider, beginning with a spear-phishing campaign to gain initial access. The attack progressed through exploitation, persistence, privilege escalation, and defense evasion before culminating in data manipulation to disrupt operations.
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: ["Phishing", "Vulnerability exploit", "Security misconfigurations and vulnerabilities", "Man in The Middle(MiTM)", "Data and Command Integrity Attack"]

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2025-43300 [CWE-XXX]: Suggested vulnerability for mobile phishing attacks
stage.description: The threat actor initiated the attack by sending spear-phishing emails with malicious Microsoft Office attachments containing macros. These emails exploited social engineering tactics to trick employees into enabling macro execution, thereby compromising endpoints.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2025-31200 [CWE-XXX]: Exploited non-production interfaces in communication infrastructure
stage.description: The attacker deployed a web-based exploit against unpatched VoIP/PBX systems, leveraging publicly accessible interfaces to execute arbitrary code and establish a foothold within the network.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: A hidden scheduled task was created to ensure the malware persists across reboots or system updates, exploiting misconfigured permissions to maintain unauthorized access.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 384: Application API Message Manipulation via Man-in-the-Middle
stage.description: The attacker manipulated API messages using a MiTM technique to escalate privileges, gaining access to administrative accounts and critical communication servers.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 636: Hiding Malicious Data or Code within Files
stage.description: Payloads and C2 traffic were encrypted and encoded to evade signature-based detection mechanisms, allowing lateral movement without triggering alerts.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Routing tables and call records were altered to disrupt communication services, while ransomware was deployed to encrypt critical data, causing operational downtime and financial losses.

Scenario.summary: The C0027 campaign targeted a communications provider through a sophisticated phishing attack, escalating privileges and evading defenses to manipulate critical data, ultimately disrupting services and causing significant operational harm.
